channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: 'Why would any American kid go into engineering if he thinks....': Fox News' Laura Ingraham asks Republican leader on his anti-H-1B bill
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: British man wrongly jailed 17 years for rape says rapist's 24-year sentence is an 'insult'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Where do you think I am from?' Indian-origin man counters racist bullying in Canada, says everyone should talk back
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Hostile missiles, drones intercepted': Kuwait, Bahrain come under Iranian attack; watch
item.description: Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones toward Kuwait and Bahrain, prompting air defense responses in both nations. The US military intercepted Iranian missiles and drones targeting the Strait of Hormuz and allies, striking Iranian radar sites in response. These actions escalate tensions between Tehran and Washington amid ongoing regional confrontations.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UAE suspends visas and bars entry for nationals of three countries amid Ebola precautions
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch: Ukrainian drones hit St Petersburg region again; Russia claims capturing village
item.description: Ukraine launched long-range drone strikes deep inside Russia, hitting a military facility near St Petersburg and an oil depot in Krasnodar. President Zelenskyy stated these attacks targeted Russian naval arsenals and bases, emphasizing Ukraine's capability to strike over 1,000 kilometers.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UAE's MoEI partners with 42 Abu Dhabi for AI hackathon to transform government services
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Weapon used was not kirpan': UK Sikh MPs distance religion from Henry Nowak murder
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'No community should live in fear': British MP backs Southampton Sikhs amid rising hostility after Vickrum Digwa's conviction
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Dubai Police Deploy AI Traffic Systems: Smart tools predict crashes; crackdown targets reckless driving
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Turkish gang member convicted over plot to murder man outside family's London restaurant after £100,000 demand
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Didn't want to be Jimmy Carter': Trump shares why US troops didn't extract Iran's uranium
item.description: President Trump revealed a shelved plan to militarily extract Iran's enriched uranium, deeming it too risky. Despite this, he asserted the US capability to seize the material, which he described as "entombed" and secure. Meanwhile, US envoys consulted specialists for upcoming nuclear talks with Tehran, though significant divisions persist.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Fact check: Did Tyla really get N3on kicked out of a Celebrity Dinner in Monaco? What really happened during the livestream
item.description: A viral social media rumor falsely claimed singer Tyla got streamer N3on removed from a celebrity dinner in Monaco after he filmed her during a livestream. The controversy exploded after several X accounts misidentified an Instagram model as the Grammy-winning artist. Posts alleged security threats and a heated confrontation, but fans later pointed out the woman in the video was not Tyla. The incident quickly became another example of how misinformation spreads online when viral clips and celebrity culture collide.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: “You and your fake husband....": Candace Owens accuses Laura Loomer of lying about her marriage in explosive social media feud
item.description: Laura Loomer and Candace Owens ignited a fierce online feud after Loomer accused Owens of using her Russia trip to prepare for possible asylum if investigated for alleged FARA violations. Loomer also speculated about Owens’ pregnancy. Owens fired back with a deeply personal response, accusing Loomer of lying about her relationship and mocking her Alaskan cruise photos. Their explosive exchange quickly went viral, drawing intense reactions across social media and further escalating tensions between the two controversial conservative personalities.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Two Romanians found guilty of stabbing TV presenter in London ‘on order of Iran’
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: How did ChudTheBuilder’s bond payment end JiDion and Alex Rosen’s partnership? Inside the growing controversy
item.description: JiDion has ended his partnership with Alex Rosen after Rosen donated $10,000 toward controversial streamer ChudTheBuilder’s bond. JiDion announced the split in his June 4 video This Is The End, saying the decision went against his values. The fallout has sparked major debate online, with supporters praising JiDion’s move and critics questioning why the split happened now.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: “They are psychopaths”: Matt Walsh erupts after Jesse Ridgway and Ashley end pregnancy over Down syndrome diagnosis
item.description: YouTuber Jesse Ridgway and his wife Ashley revealed that they ended a pregnancy after receiving a Down syndrome diagnosis for their unborn child. The announcement led to heavy criticism from political commentator Matt Walsh, who condemned their decision on X. Ridgway defended the choice, calling the experience traumatic and saying the couple believed it was best for their family.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: $24 billion 'trust test' for Trump? Khamenei aide seeks frozen assets, warns war could take 'another dimension'
item.description: A potential peace deal between the US and Iran hinges on the release of $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets, according to military advisor Mohsen Rezaei. He stated that the ball is in President Trump's court to break the negotiation deadlock, proposing the funds as a test of trust. Rezaei also warned of expanded military responses if attacked again.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: British actor Anthony Head dies at 72, leaving a legacy across Buffy, Ted Lasso and Merlin
item.description: Veteran British actor Anthony Head, celebrated for his roles in Buffy the Vampire Slayer and Ted Lasso, has passed away at 72. His career, spanning over four decades, included iconic advertising campaigns and beloved television dramas. Head's family confirmed his peaceful death, remembering him as an extraordinary father whose extensive work will be remembered by generations.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Over $580,000 forfeited in Australian visa fraud crackdown; man jailed for submitting false migration documents
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
